body:
Kicks off the note-taking app with a working, tested foundation for all three requested pieces.

## What's here
- **User auth** (`src/auth/auth.js`) — registration, login, scrypt password hashing, HMAC-signed expiring bearer tokens, and an `authenticate()` middleware helper. Closes #1.
- **Notes CRUD API** (`src/notes/notes.js`) — per-user create/read/update/delete with ownership enforcement (users can't touch each other's notes). Closes #2.
- **Sync service** (`src/sync/sync.js`) — `pull` (changes since a timestamp) and `push` (client batch) with last-write-wins conflict resolution. Closes #3.
- **HTTP API** (`src/server.js`) — routes wired on top of the modules using Node built-ins only (no runtime deps yet).
- **Store** (`src/lib/store.js`) — in-memory data layer, intended to be swapped for a real DB.

## Tests
`npm test` (`node --test`) — 19 passing: unit tests per module plus an HTTP integration test covering register -> create -> list -> sync, auth rejection, and cross-user isolation. The original `items` fixture test still passes.

## Deliberately deferred
Real database persistence, refresh tokens / revocation, rate limiting, input-size limits, and richer sync conflict strategies. Left as a draft for review of the shape before we build it out.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Scaffold note-taking app: auth, notes CRUD, sync
